summary: A reconnect loop happens when the League client believes a match is active but the game process cannot successfully connect, launch, or stay attached to the session.
likelyCauses: Temporary Riot service or regional routing issues.
Local firewall, VPN, DNS, or router problems blocking game traffic.
Damaged client files or a stuck League/Riot process.
Graphics driver or overlay conflict preventing the game process from opening correctly.
safeFixes: Check Riot service status for League of Legends before changing local settings.
Close League and Riot Client completely from Task Manager, then relaunch.
Restart the PC and router to clear stale network sessions.
Temporarily disconnect VPNs or proxies and try a standard connection.
Allow League of Legends and Riot Client through Windows Firewall using Windows settings.
Run the Riot Client repair flow or reinstall the client from Riot's official download page if repair does not work.
riskyFixesToAvoid: Do not share account credentials while troubleshooting.
Do not download unofficial client patchers.
Do not disable firewall or antivirus permanently.
Do not delete random Windows system files.
